exercises.code:
# skeptic/B1: packet-only for S20-T2-A-E3
from openpyxl import Workbook
from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill

def header_style(ws, cell):
    c = ws[cell]
    c.font = Font(bold=True)
    c.fill = PatternFill("solid", fgColor="1F4E79")

wb = Workbook()
ws = wb.active
header_style(ws, "A1")
print(ws["A1"].font.bold)

exercises.code:
# skeptic/B1: packet-only for S20-T4-B-E2
import hashlib

def build(rows):
    lines = ["region,monto"] + [f"{r},{m}" for r, m in sorted(rows)]
    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"

a = build([("Lima", 10), ("Cusco", 5)])
b = build([("Cusco", 5), ("Lima", 10)])
print(hashlib.sha1(a.encode()).hexdigest() == hashlib.sha1(b.encode()).hexdigest())

selfcheck.chosen_text: No; suele devolver la fórmula o cache si existe
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [B1/Skeptic] Skeptic: «fórmulas vs valores cacheados» — openpyxl by default does not evaluate Excel formulas; you get formula string or cached value. Prefer Python calc for pipelines.
[B1/Skeptic] Skeptic: «fechas, locales, celdas combinadas» — Merged cells: write the anchor (top-left); non-anchor may read None. Demos print non_anchor D1 as None.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Estados de batch, conciliación y backups
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [B1/Skeptic] Skeptic: batch/backups theory + iDo — manifest lists ok/corrupt/locked, conciliation, backup path. Audit surface is batch status + reconcile + backups, not font color.
[B1/Skeptic] Skeptic: «backups, idempotencia y pruebas» — Idempotencia: re-ejecutar con mismos inputs produce mismo resultado lógico. Not random totals.
